name: Spanish
modelReview: blind 3-persona Sonnet panel + round-trip over the spanning set (34 patterns, 2026-06-24), re-tested against the current bar (the original 2026-06-15 score was the retired Gemma cross-family method). Round-trip clean (meaning preserved). Naturalness fixes landed test-first via blind-panel-decided forms: the missing article in 'el minuto X y el segundo Y', 'y también' for a range plus an isolated hour, the OR-scope 'en diciembre …, ya sea el día 31 o un viernes' (shared month+time fronted, union last), seconds nested into a fixed clock time, and a second cadence anchored with 'del minuto M'. 29/34 items at median naturalness ≥ 4. Documented residuals below.
note: BETA — model-validated by a blind 3-persona Sonnet panel (round-trip-clean). Known residuals, not yet resolved: (1) the repeated 'a las' in a mixed-article clock list is the cost of rendering 'a la 01:00' correctly — eliding breaks the article; (2) a minute window confined to specific hours ('cada minuto del 0 al 30, a las …') reads restrictive-vs-additive — the 'solo' marker was tried and the blind re-review found it misread, so it was reverted; (3) a minute window crossed with an hour step ('cada minuto del 0 al 30, cada dos horas') has no clean idiom; (4) restricted-month OR unions where an arm is a list/range (e.g. '0 12 1 6-9 MON-FRI') keep the older trailing-month form — the 'ya sea … o' fix is scoped to single/single arms, since broadening would coin Spanish no panel has validated; (5) the minute-range register ('del 0 al 29') is inherent to expressing cron precisely. Graduates to stable only on fluent-human review.
